scenarios.query:
Review this change:
```python
class Subscription:
    def __init__(self):
        self.state = "trial"        # "trial" | "active" | "cancelled"
        self.paid_until = None      # set when active
        self.cancel_reason = None   # set when cancelled
        self.card_last4 = ""        # "" until a card is attached
```
expected_behavior: Flags that illegal state combinations are representable (e.g. cancelled with paid_until set, active with no card)
Flags the stringly-typed state and the None/empty-string sentinels
Recommends a tagged union / state machine (or enum + per-state data) so each state carries exactly its own fields
Flags the mutation of another object's internal state from outside (feature envy / poor encapsulation)
Recommends asking the object for what's needed (e.g. customer.contactEmail(), customer.markOverdueNotified()) instead of reaching through
Recognizes parse-don't-validate: input is parsed once at the boundary into an immutable type that can't hold an invalid value
Does not suggest adding speculative methods, interfaces, or abstractions to the small value object
